hypothesis: Polymers with the same total amine density that incorporate a moderate branching density (25–40% of monomer units as branch points), increase tertiary-amine fraction to 20–35%, and include hydrophobic spacers of 2–4 methylene units between amine sites will exhibit at least 20% higher equilibrium CO2 uptake at 298 K and 0.4 mbar under 50% relative humidity than equivalent linear polymers lacking these features due to reduced competitive H2O binding.
